metadata.title: Nintendo's Family-Friendly Platform Strategy
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929; SPARK #D97757 one accent.
metadata.author: Seth Brown
metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Seth Brown — Humanitarians AI Fellow. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Nintendo's shared screen makes play a shared experience rather than isolating each player. There are no 'you lost your progress' mechanics to punish weaker or younger players. Each hazard is legible: one Goomba, one pit, and the mushroom is a reward, not a gate. The aesthetic is legible rather than childish, using a bright palette, readable silhouettes, and clean geometry.

artifactLines: Nintendo's shared screen makes play a shared experience rather than isolating each player.
There are no 'you lost your progress' mechanics to punish weaker or younger players.
Each hazard is legible: one Goomba, one pit, and the mushroom is a reward, not a gate.
The aesthetic is legible rather than childish, using a bright palette, readable silhouettes, and clean geometry.

beats.narration_text: The design reads I loved were that shared screen equals shared experience and that the mushroom is a reward, not a gate. I want to apply them. Help me: (1) explain the difference between a reward and a gate using the Goomba, pit, and mushroom hazards, (2) show how 'no lost-progress' mechanics change who can play, and (3) redesign one punishing level rule into a family-friendly one using these principles.

props.command: The design reads I loved were that shared screen equals shared experience and that the mushroom is a reward, not a gate. I want to apply them. Help me: (1) explain the difference between a reward and a gate using the Goomba, pit, and mushroom hazards, (2) show how 'no lost-progress' mechanics change who can play, and (3) redesign one punishing level rule into a family-friendly one using these principles.
output: Ceiling is uncapped (200cc, 3-star files, speedrun routing)

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Paste this into Claude — Take the central idea from 'Nintendo's Family-Friendly Platform Strategy' and give me the three most actionable takeaways — one… That's the prompt that actually tests whether you got it. Run it and see where it surprises you.

props.command: Take the central idea from 'Nintendo's Family-Friendly Platform Strategy' and give me the three most actionable takeaways — one sentence each. Then name the failure mode I'm most likely to hit.
